sections.paragraph:
ἀποτινάσσω 1 aor. ἀπετίναξα; fut. mid. ἀποτινάξομαι Judg 16:20 cod. A; pf. 3 sg. ἀποτετίνακται 1 Kgm 10:2 (τινάσσω ‘shake, brandish’; Eur., Bacch. 253; Galen: CMG V 4, 2 p. 458, 8=VI 821 K.; LXX) shake off τὶ, of a snake which has bitten a hand τὸ θηρίον εἰς τὸ πῦρ Ac 28:5. τὸν κονιορτὸν (Amulet of Parisinus 2316 leaf 318 verso ff: Rtzst., Poim. 297f κονιορτὸν ἀποτινάξαι) ἀπὸ τ. ποδῶν ἀ. shake the dust fr. one’s feet Lk 9:5 (s. on ἐκτινάσσω 1).—Of St. Paul’s beheading καὶ ἀπετ[ίναξεν … ὡς δὲ ὁ σπεκουλάτωρ ἀπ]ε̣τ̣ί̣ν̣α|ξεν αὐτοῦ τὴν κ̣[εφαλήν] AcPl Ha 10, 26f [the executioner] beheaded him [… But after the executioner] had beheaded him.—DELG s.v. τινάσσω.

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ff ff = (and) following

Rtzst., Rtzst., Poim. = Poimandres

  1. fr. = from

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 124
